docs.title: База знаний по takeoff и estimating
docs.text:
Workflow, чек-листы, правила COM/EWP, советы из реальных правок и таблицы по walls, framing, sheathing, hangers — в одном месте.

Старт Советы и важные вещи Quality Checklist

docs.title: С чего начать
docs.text:
• Workflow

    Полный путь: подготовить PDF → разметить takeoff → экспорт в Excel → проверка перед отправкой.

    Открыть

  • Takeoff Structure

    Как раскладывать листы, секции, scale и почему важна единая структура для COM/EWP.

    Открыть

  • Quality Checklist

    Проверка перед сдачей: формулы, waste, FRT, hangers, метал, нестыковки чертежей.

    Открыть

  • Client Rules

    Кто включает метал, кого считать «by others», особенности WM / Timberline / EBS / Probuild.

    Открыть

docs.text:

    Sill / Unit / Exterior / Corridor / Demising / Gable / Parapet / Shaft / Furring / Corners.

  • Roof Framing

    Roof Framing

  • Sheathing

    Sheathing

  • SQFTs

    Basement, 1st-5th, Loft, Roof, Deck, Porch, Balcony, Cantilevered.

    SQFTs

  • :material-deck:{ .lg .middle } Deck / Porch / Balcony

    Railing, Balcony Trims, Anchor Bolts.

    Открыть

  • Interior Trims

    Base, Casing, Crown, Door/Window, Room Schedule.

    Interior Trims

docs.title: Справочник
docs.text:
• Советы и важные вещи

    Реальные правила из правок: rim, blocking, FRT, DHU/ITS, studs, sheathing, doors, trims, output.

    Открыть

  • Hangers

    ITS / HU / HIT / WP / MIT / GLTV — таблицы выбора по joist и beam.

    Hangers

  • Formulas & Factors

    Waste-факторы, формулы spacing, повторные коэффициенты — что не множить дважды.

    Formulas

  • Source Map

    Откуда что взято, что ещё не импортировано (Trello/Confluence pending).

    Source Map

docs.title: Схемы и иллюстрации
docs.text:
Сюда подгружаются картинки и диаграммы по разделам — wall sections, framing details, roof connections, hanger выбор. Пока заглушки; добавляются по мере импорта.

:material-image-plus: Wall section — exterior 2x6 :material-image-plus: Floor framing detail — joist + rim :material-image-plus: Roof framing — ridge + valley :material-image-plus: Hanger selection chart :material-image-plus: Sheathing zones :material-image-plus: Interior trim — base / casing / crown

Как добавлять картинки и схемы

Полный гайд с шаблонами — Картинки и схемы: <figure> с подписью, две колонки «текст + схема», галереи, schema на всю ширину, картинка внутри admonition.

Поиск (Ctrl/Cmd + K) индексируется на русском и английском. Исходники и edit-режим: github.com/artrmiys/knowledge-base-e-wood.

docs.title: Советы и важные вещи
docs.text: Ключевая страница wiki: правила, собранные из реальных правок и фидбэка по проектам. Многих из этих вещей нет в общих гайдах по estimating — это локальная память команды.

docs.title: Rim Board
docs.text:
Wrong Correct 1-3/4 LVL Rim when not specified 11-7/8" Rim or product marked as assumed Splitting rim into 16' pieces Keep rim in LFT with 1.05 factor
  • If LSL is called out, write 11-7/8" LSL Rim.
  • 1-3/4 LVL Rim is used only when something attaches to it for strength, such as deck or corridor frame.
  • Rim is needed at roof TJI too, not only at floors.

docs.title: Blocking
docs.text:
• Walls 10' and above need two rows of blocking.
  • Blocking stays in LFT unless the output specifically needs pieces.
  • Use 10% waste for framing elements such as drywall blocking, rim, and upper walls.
  • For top chord bearing trusses, do not add 2x4 ribbon board; use blocking between trusses, often (2) 2x6.

docs.title: FRT
docs.text: Element Rule Exterior blocking FRT if exterior wall material is FRT Parapets FRT if exterior walls are FRT Subfloor perimeter Check 2' or 4' FRT perimeter notes Demising shear wall sheathing Regular sheathing unless schedule says FRT Stair / CMU two-hour walls Often FRT; verify details

docs.title: DHU / DGU vs ITS
docs.text:
• DHU/DGU only where joists hang over firewall conditions at stairs, elevators, or shafts.
  • Regular demising conditions where gypsum stops under the floor usually use ITS.
  • DHU can cost several times more than ITS, so verify details before listing.
  • Mark Stair / Elevator / Shaft hangers separately so review is clear.

docs.title: Studs
docs.text:
Spacing Factor Waste 16" o.c. 1:1 10% 24" o.c. 0.5 25%
  • Use exact heights on large COM jobs: 9'0-3/8", 9'1-1/8", etc.
  • Example math: 11'1" wall - 22" truss - 3/4" subfloor = 9'2" stud.
  • Corridor 2x4 staggered means two rows at 16" o.c.; plates should be 2x6.
  • Bearing walls on lower floors may require double studs by structural notes.

docs.title: Sheathing
docs.text:
• 19/32" equals 5/8", not 1/2".
  • Exterior sheathing follows Arch / energy / Zip notes unless Structural gives stronger non-Zip requirement.
  • Interior sheathing follows Structural.
  • Zip sheathing on exterior walls supersedes structural sheathing notes, but keep a note.
  • Optional walls may need full-height sheathing while loose/box sheathing stays box only.

docs.title: Commonly Missed Items
docs.text:
• 1/2" plywood underlayment per floor assembly.
  • Piggy truss sleepers: often 2x6 between upper truss parts.
  • 1/4" Densedeck or glass mat cover board at flat roofs.
  • Additional rigid XPS layer.
  • Drywall ledger: 2x4 at demising walls both sides and exterior walls one side where parallel with framing.
  • Chute shaft wall A201/A806, wall type 7A.
  • A35 clips at shearwall connections.
  • Jamb blocking for all windows and interior doors.
  • Kitchen and bath blocking.
  • Holdowns per S-details.

docs.title: Doors
docs.text:
• Unit entry doors from corridors are fire rated.
  • Use labels such as 3070 Entry, 2670 FCW, or 3070 HM C-lbl.
  • Door hardware numbers usually do not belong in the takeoff list.
  • Interior door jamb trim can use casing divided by 2 where that is the local estimating method.

docs.title: Interior Trims
docs.text:
• Room schedule: include only wood base / Wd; exclude tile base.
  • Corridors, lobbies, and other common areas should be listed separately because trim type can differ from units.
  • Crowns should be included when interior trim scope is active.
  • If trims are not specified, write that they are not specified instead of inventing a trim type.

docs.title: Client Metal Rule
docs.text:
Include metal Exclude / by others WM EBS Timberline Probuild Littleton Triangle Interstate Bliffert

When excluded, still note metal locations as by others.

docs.title: Formatting and Output
docs.text:
• Do not combine floors even if they are the same; copy data separately.
  • Add a note when a floor frame is identical to another floor.
  • Stair treads: list 2x12 Tread and add 1x8 Riser.
  • Do not count nails; list bolts and Simpson screws where required.
  • Do not leave copied detail labels uncorrected.
  • Use Schedule, not Shedule.
  • Floor labels use 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, etc.
  • If material comes from a separate specification PDF, add a note such as per customer note.
  • Remove unused rows/items from output, except formulas that still drive wall calculations.
  • Do not swap count and length columns for joists or similar repeated members.
  • For duplex / repeated-building jobs, check multipliers twice so half a building is not missed.

docs.title: Joist / Stud Spacing Factors
docs.text:
Spacing Factor 12" o.c. 1.4667 16" o.c. 1.1 24" o.c. 0.625

For studs at 16" o.c., boss feedback says use 1:1 plus 10% waste rather than 0.75. For 24" o.c., use 0.5 plus 25% waste.

docs.title: Rim and Blocking
docs.text: Item Unit Factor Rim Board LFT 1.05 Blocking LFT no factor if continuous Ribbons / ledgers / bracing pieces use 16' ceiling when required

docs.title: COM Waste
docs.text:
• Use 1.1 for formulas unless the source value already includes the 1.1.
  • Do not double-count waste through chained formulas.

docs.title: Shaft Walls
docs.text:
CH channels vertical  = LFT * 0.5 * 1.1 / 12
J-channels horizontal = LFT * 2 / 10 * 1.1 / 10
Shaft panels          = LFT * 0.5 * 1.1, listed as 2x12

docs.title: Dropped Ceiling Metal Joists
docs.text: 12" C-type metal joists = Area * 0.75 * 1.1 LF

docs.title: Blocking Formulas
docs.text:
Flat 48" o.c.     = CEILING(G * 12 / 48 * 2 * 1.1 / D, 1)
Diagonal 48" o.c. = CEILING(G * 12 / 48 * 2.5 * 1.1 / D, 1)

docs.title: Kitchen and Bath Blocking
docs.text: Location Rule Kitchen 2x6 blocking, 4 pieces of 14' per kitchen Bathroom 2x6 blocking, 1 piece of 14' per bathroom

docs.title: Hangers
docs.text: Fast hanger selection: start with mount condition, then depth, skew, and firewall condition.

docs.title: Core Selection Rules
docs.text:
• Joists 16" and below can use ITS when top-flange hanger is appropriate.
  • Joists over 16" use HIT hangers such as HIT420.
  • ITS cannot be skewed.
  • Skewed top mount: use WP / HUTF family.
  • Skewed face mount: use SUR / HSUR family.
  • DHU/DGU is only for double-gypsum/firewall conditions; verify details.

docs.title: Quick Checks
docs.text: Question Why it matters Is the joist deeper than 16"? Usually changes ITS to HIT Is the hanger skewed? ITS is not skewable; use WP/HUTF or SUR/HSUR family Is it face mount or top flange? Changes HU/HUC/IUS vs ITS/WP/GLTV Is there double gypsum/firewall? DHU/DGU only belongs at those conditions Is it a beam, glulam, or Cedar member? May require HHUS/HGUS/CBH/CJTZ special item

docs.title: Reading ITS
docs.text:
ITS2.37/11.88

  • ITS = I-joist top-flange hanger.
  • 2.37 = joist flange width, about 2 5/16".
  • 11.88 = joist height, 11 7/8".

docs.title: Special
docs.text:
• CJTZ for Cedar beams/posts.
  • CBH2.37X9.75C-KT for glulam.
  • A35 clips at shearwall connections when required by general notes.
  • After converting repeated hanger counts, re-check the hanger quantity. A framing repeat change can double the hanger count too.
  • Simpson HDU4-SDS2.5: use double when it sits on a wall at upper floors; use single when it sits directly on an LVL/steel beam.

  • Exterior Walls
  • Unit / Interior Walls
  • Sill Plates
  • Box Sheathing

Key points:

  • Track wall height by basement, first floor, second floor, loft/attic, chimney, and dormers.
  • Count Sill Plates and Shelf Plates with visible notes.
  • For Sill Plates under KD Plates, note the two lower boards and P.T. lower plate.
  • Split basement/first/second/attic walls by 2x4, 2x6, 2x8, exterior, furring, flat furring, knee, chimney, dormer, garage, and interior conditions.
  • Put Studs PreCut, corners, corner studs, extra plates, and garage door quantity where applicable.
  • Check hidden box sheathing in sections.

docs.title: Gables Page Summary
docs.text:
Imported into:

  • Gable Walls
  • Gable Sheathing

Key points:

  • Track exterior, interior, porch, truss, and dormer gables.
  • Gables can be 2x4 or 2x6.
  • Track sheathing area.
  • For truss gables, remove plates and studs from the left/output side.
  • Dormers should be recorded with wall scope.
  • Gable stud height should be visible on the left/output side.

docs.title: Trello Source Map
docs.text:
Эта страница показывает полный Trello coverage: какие доски выгружены, сколько карточек/картинок есть в raw archive, и куда эти правила связаны в wiki. Raw exports лежат в imports/ и не публикуются на GitHub Pages.

Карточки с приватными/tooling ссылками редактируются в публичной таблице, но остаются в локальном raw archive.

  • Private/tooling link cards stay raw-only.

docs.title: Client Rules
docs.text: These rules come from local Obsidian/E-Wood notes and boss feedback. Recheck against current specs when a project contradicts the default.

docs.title: Metal Studs
docs.text:
Client Default metal-stud treatment WM Include / show metal Timberline Include / show metal Littleton Include / show metal EBS By others / do not include Probuild By others / do not include Triangle By others / do not include Interstate By others / do not include Bliffert By others / do not include

Even when excluded, note where metal studs/floors are used and mark them as by others.

docs.title: Bosfa-Type Notes
docs.text:
• Room schedule: include wood base only; exclude tile base.
  • Door list: hardware numbers are not needed, but material and fire rating are.
  • Example labels: 2670 FCW, 3070 HM C-lbl.
  • Deflection tracks only at full-height walls touching ceiling above.
  • Dropped soffits use regular top tracks.

docs.title: Capital / EWP
docs.text: Capital-type EWP jobs usually list only EWP materials: joists, beams, and associated hangers. Do not count ordinary 2x material unless the scope says so.

docs.title: How to Use
docs.text: Эта wiki должна быть быстрой рабочей памятью: открыл страницу, проверил правило, применил в takeoff tool или Excel.

docs.title: Приоритет источников
docs.text:
1. Реальные drawings/specs текущего проекта.
  2. Structural details и schedules.
  3. Arch plans, assemblies, RCP, wall types, energy notes.
  4. Client-specific rules из этой wiki.
  5. Советы и важные вещи.

Если правило из wiki конфликтует с drawings/specs, не подменяй проект. Запиши вопрос или note в output.

docs.title: Как читать страницы
docs.text:
• Start — процесс, структура, checklist.
  • Work Types — логика COM, EWP/Capital, Residential.
  • Work — предметные страницы по разделам takeoff.
  • Reference — правила, таблицы, формулы, source map.

docs.title: Как обновлять
docs.text:
• Добавляй новые правила в точную предметную страницу и в Советы и важные вещи, если это feedback.
  • Не храни приватные ссылки, emails, UIDs, salary history и credentials.
  • Если правило относится к конкретному клиенту, обнови Client Rules.
  • Если правило повторяется в разных местах, держи короткую версию на topic page и полную версию в reference page.

docs.title: Картинки и схемы
docs.text: Шаблоны, как вставлять картинки и схемы единообразно. Это конструкторская шпаргалка — визуала будет много, поэтому держим все вставки в одном стиле.

docs.title: Куда класть файлы
docs.text:
docs/
└── assets/
    └── images/
        ├── walls/
        │   ├── exterior-2x6-section.png
        │   └── ...
        ├── framing/
        ├── roof/
        └── hangers/
  • Раскладка по папкам = по разделу wiki, чтобы не превратилось в свалку.
  • Имена файлов: латиница, нижний регистр, тире (exterior-2x6-section.png).
  • PNG/SVG для схем, JPG/WEBP для фото. Свыше 500 KB — по возможности ужимать.

docs.title: Правила оформления
docs.text:
• Каждой схеме — короткая подпись (что, где смотреть, какой проект).
  • Если схема из конкретного проекта — добавь номер проекта в подпись и не публикуй приватные части (имена клиентов, цены, личные данные).
  • Не вставляй скриншоты с полем «Email/UID/Password» — обрезай до содержания.
  • Большие PDF не вставляй картинкой — лучше отдельной страницей с pdf.js или ссылкой на raw-файл.

docs.title: Important Changes
docs.text:
Important Changes — use as a visual QA map before output.

Source board: https://trello.com/b/wDztpnZg/изменения-очень-важно

Imported from the logged-in browser session and full Trello API export: 75 cards and 20 attachments. This page is the visual shelf for high-priority rule changes.

docs.title: Visual Rules
docs.text: Rule / reminder Topic Source Suspended ceiling formula: S*0.75*2 + S*0.75*1*0.75 in LFT. Formula Trello Balcony framing: 2-ply and 2 layers of sleepers. Balcony / Deck Trello For roof with AJS Rafters, count rafters manually instead of Rake when the condition is like an exposed floor overhang. Roof / Rake Trello Sound insulation only when insulation is actually required. Insulation Trello Ribbonboard only for trusses. Floor / Truss Trello Count every 10' as blocking for Trusses - Bracing 2x6. Blocking Trello If a post is not specified, leave the correct visible note instead of guessing. Post Trello Washers: do not leave the spacing unchanged; update the spacing. Anchor / Washers Trello Write LVL without quotation marks. Naming Trello If EWP by others but one LVL beam is in the floor, write a note. EWP / Notes Trello Fix extra spaces in material names. Output QA Trello Cantilevered needs that label. SQFT / Label Trello Use the precut formula because manual errors are common. Formula / Precut Trello

docs.title: Text Rules From Full Export
docs.text:
These cards did not always have useful screenshots, but the card titles are the rule. Keep them as QA prompts before output.

Rule / reminder Topic Source Keep Porch Trims in one place so review is not split across unrelated rows. Porch / Deck Trello Write Ledger and Box as separate rows. Ledger / Box Trello Remove unused rows/items, except wall formulas that still drive the workbook. Output QA Trello Always check the specification and write a note when material comes from specs. Specs / Notes Trello Distinguish EWP Floor from All EWP; do not treat one as the other. EWP Trello Copy repeated areas carefully, especially porches and decks. QA Trello 8'-1" studs are not 9' precut; fix the visible wall heights/output. Studs / Precut Trello Do not swap joist count and joist length. Joist Trello Measure first, then write the value immediately. QA Trello For headers, (3) means three plies; do not enter one board for a triple header. Headers Trello After converting repeated hanger counts, re-check hanger quantities too. Hangers Trello Vinyl Soffits belong in SQFT. SQFT / Soffit Trello If materials are in a separate specification PDF, add per customer note. Specs / Notes Trello Long 2x ceiling joists may be split and imply supporting vertical joists. Ceiling Joist Trello For Tri-Force and beams with steel plates, write nearest foot sizes, not inches. Beam Trello Do not create Posts / Post Caps for every built-up beam; only when actual 6x6, 4x4, etc. posts are called out. Post Trello Bracing is always 2x4 unless a detail says otherwise. Bracing Trello If a 28' wide house has a dropped beam, joists may be longer than 14'; verify span labels. Joist Trello Use the spelling Schedule and floor labels like 1st, 2nd, 3rd. Output QA Trello Cross bridging for I-joists / TB27: length * 2 * 12 / 16 pcs. Joist / Blocking Trello For attic EWP by others, write note LVLs by others. EWP / Notes Trello On duplex jobs, check multipliers twice; missing half a building is a common failure. QA Trello Box sheathing: attic floor height is added only to the first height. Box Sheathing Trello Attic subfloor can be extra plate + box = subfloor. Box / Subfloor Trello TJI 9 1/2 does not use 360 / 560 series. Joist Trello If Soffit Eve Vinyl is shown, enter it in SQFT. SQFT / Soffit Trello For AJS Rafters, count rafters manually instead of Rake when it behaves like an exposed floor overhang. Roof / Rake Trello Simpson HDU4-SDS2.5: double when it sits on a wall at upper floors; single when it sits on LVL/steel. Holdowns Trello

docs.text:
This wiki is a MkDocs Material project.

python -m mkdocs serve
python -m mkdocs build --strict

GitHub Actions deploys to gh-pages on push to main.

docs.title: Update Rules
docs.text:
• Keep pages short enough to scan during work.
  • Put reusable rules in Reference.
  • Put task-specific instructions in the closest Work page.
  • Prefer tables for selection rules, factors, and client exceptions.
  • Add assumptions explicitly; do not hide them in prose.

docs.title: Redaction Rules
docs.text:
Do not publish:

  • Employee emails or UIDs.
  • Salary history or private payroll tables.
  • Dropbox links and private chat links.
  • Credentials, API keys, server passwords, or personal contact data.

If a private source contains a useful rule, rewrite the rule without the private identifier.

docs.title: Файловое хранилище
docs.text:
• Всё хранится в MEGA (облако).
  • Готовый PlanSwift и финальные файлы — только в облаке.

docs.title: Проверяющий
docs.text:
1. Открывает PlanSwift и Excel, проверяет, пишет ошибки в облаке (в соответствующей заметке/файле).
  2. Если ошибки есть — в Trello ставит красную метку, убирает зелёную, снимает даты.
  3. В Telegram пишет: номер дома + «нужно/не нужно править».
  4. Всегда указывает, где править Excel: [локальный] или [облачный]. Пишет, какие правки уже сделал.
  5. Если срочно — сам ставит дедлайн и указывает: правим всё или отправляем как есть (с коротким описанием, что сделано).

docs.title: Worker
docs.text:
1. Открывает PlanSwift и начинает правки, параллельно задаёт вопросы.
  2. В Trello — жёлтая метка и примерная дата готовности.
  3. Excel править.
  4. После завершения правок — зелёная метка.

docs.title: Итерации
docs.text:
• Цикл может повториться 2–3 раза до полного согласования.
  • На разбор ошибок резервируй 30–60 минут.

docs.title: Финальная проверка («было/стало»)
docs.text: См. Quality Checklist → Финальная проверка.

docs.title: Quality Checklist
docs.text: Use this before sending a job.

docs.title: Global
docs.text:
• Scale verified on each drawing set used for takeoff.
  • Arch, Structural, specs, schedules, wall types, RCP pages checked.
  • All new formulas tested immediately.
  • Identical floors noted, but materials still listed per floor.
  • No double 1.1 waste from formulas feeding other formulas.
  • Notes added where assumptions are made.

docs.title: COM Critical Checks
docs.text:
1. FRT scope checked in specs and details.
  2. If exterior wall materials are FRT, exterior blocking and parapets are FRT too.
  3. Multi-layer sheathing, underlayment, sound membrane checked.
  4. Edge sheathing around floor/roof perimeter checked.
  5. Gypcrete floors checked for double bottom plates.
  6. Rigid insulation checked.
  7. Interior sheathing and holdowns checked.
  8. Parapet inside/outside sheathing checked.
  9. Piggy trusses and sleepers checked.
  10. Flat roof curb blocking checked.
  11. Exact stud heights calculated.
  12. S-details followed when present.
  13. Exterior sheathing checked against Arch/energy notes.
  14. Window blocking checked, especially Zip R or insulation.
  15. 1x3 PT strapping checked for Hardi paneling.
  16. Exterior buildouts checked as stick framed.
  17. Shaft walls checked.
  18. Dropped soffits checked on RCP pages.
  19. Soffit plywood under roof trusses checked.
  20. Under podium / existing conditions checked; do not assume panels.

docs.title: Frequent Misses
docs.text:
• Rim at roof TJI.
  • Two rows of blocking for walls 10' and above.
  • A35 clips at shearwall-to-shearwall connections.
  • Holdowns per details.
  • Jamb blocking for windows and interior doors.
  • Kitchen blocking: 2x6, 4 pieces of 14' per kitchen.
  • Bath blocking: 2x6, 1 piece of 14' per bathroom.
  • Densedeck or glass mat protection board at flat roofs.
  • Extra rigid XPS layer.
  • Metal studs marked by others when excluded by client.
  • Interior trims checked against room schedule.
  • Tile base excluded when only wood base is needed.
  • Crowns included when the trim scope is active.

docs.title: Финальная проверка «было/стало»
docs.text:
Перед отправкой готового takeoff проверяй пять файлов параллельно:

  1. Открыть готовый PlanSwift (с финальными правками).
  2. Открыть свой локальный Excel «до» (или из папки old, если есть).
  3. Открыть готовый Excel из Example.
  4. Сравнить Excel «было/стало»: оформление, что добавлено/удалено, формулировки.
  5. Сравнить PDF «было/стало»: оформление, что добавлено/удалено, формулировки.

На разбор ошибок и финальную проверку резервируй 30–60 минут — этот этап не пропускать.

docs.title: Цикл правок (worker side)
docs.text:
• Открыть PlanSwift и начинать правки, параллельно задавать вопросы.
  • В Trello — жёлтая метка + примерная дата готовности.
  • Excel править.
  • После завершения правок — зелёная метка.
  • Цикл может повториться 2–3 раза до полного согласования.

Готовые файлы (PlanSwift, Excel) — всегда хранятся в облаке (MEGA), не локально.

docs.title: Naming Notes
docs.text:
• Keep floors separate even when identical.
  • If a floor is identical, add a note such as 4th floor frame is identical to 3rd floor, but still list materials separately.
  • Panelized wall jobs need different counting rules; see COM Commercial.
  • Interior trims should be separated from exterior trims and gypsum.
  • The old Tilda source had many menu items as labels without active links. Current confirmed active work pages were Walls and Gables.

docs.title: Vertical Constructions — что входит
docs.text:
Вертикалка ≈ 50% здания, важно ничего не пропустить:

  • Несущие и ненесущие стены: Ext, Unit, Corr, Demising walls.
  • Фронтоны — Gables.
  • Наружная и внутренняя обшивка: Plywood, OSB, siding, gypsum.
  • Обшивка торцов перекрытия — Floor Height.
  • Обшивка торцов перекрытия — Eve Heel / Truss Heel.
  • Ветроизоляция — Tyvek.
  • Утеплитель — Insulation.

docs.title: Структура файла в PlanSwift
docs.text: В PlanSwift сначала идут общие layers (без счёта), потом — группы по этажам и типам. Общая идея — каждая ячейка xxx — это место для своей цифры.

docs.title: Background layers (не считаются)
docs.text:
• wall type, beam schedule, shear wall schedule
  • details struct, details arch
  • rcp, sections, others

docs.title: Группы (порядок в файле)
docs.text:
• walls + gables: units, base, 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, roof, parapet, el gables (×2), wall materials (insulation etc.).
  • sqfts + balconies + porch: 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th.
  • roof - eve rakes: roof.
  • framing + headers: base, 1st, 2nd, 3rd.
  • balconies / porches: balconies, porches.
  • shear walls / holdowns / ties: shear walls, shear, holddowns.
  • siding / trims: siding (×2), trims (×2).
  • interior trims, drywall.

docs.text: Сокращение Что f foundation — фундамент 1st first floor 2nd second floor 3rd third floor 5th–8th fifth to eighth floors el elevation — фасад sec section — разрез sec d section details rcp reflected ceiling plan wt wall type u units ext exterior int interior dem demising cor corridor str stair

docs.title: 1. Подготовить PDF
docs.text:
1. Импортировать PDF.
  2. Переименовать листы и разложить их по структуре.
  3. Установить scale и проверить его по известному размеру на чертеже.
  4. Просмотреть Arch + Structural + specs перед массовой разметкой.
  5. Отметить сомнительные места notes, чтобы не забыть при Excel output.

docs.title: 2. Разметка takeoff
docs.text:
Базовый порядок для COM:

  1. Vertical Constructions: Exterior, Corridor, Demising, Unit/Interior.
  2. Openings.
  3. Sheathing.
  4. SQFT.
  5. Floor Framing: Post, Beam, Joist, Details.
  6. Roof Framing.
  7. Sheathing and Misc.
  8. Deck / Porch / Balcony.
  9. Ties / Connectors.

docs.title: 3. Правила разметки
docs.text:
• Beams отмечать top-down / left-right.
  • Длина beam зависит от точки опирания.
  • Beams 8' и длиннее округлять до ближайших 2'.
  • Beams меньше 8' оставлять без округления.
  • Joists внутри одного run продолжать одним spacing top-down или left-right.
  • Не начинать spacing заново от внутренней балки.
  • Удалять joist только если он попадает прямо на beam примерно в пределах 2".

docs.title: 4. Export в Excel
docs.text:
• Экспортировать takeoff quantities.
  • Перенести в template.
  • Разнести категории: Beams, Joists, Details, Walls, Sheathing, SQFT.
  • Проверять новые формулы сразу, а не после полного job.
  • Не применять 1.1 повторно, если формула уже ссылается на данные с waste.

docs.title: 5. Перед отправкой
docs.text: Пройти Quality Checklist. Для больших COM jobs делать PDF sketch/markups, если есть риск забыть детали.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Anchor bolts, holdown anchors, deck/balcony attachment bolts, and washers where details require them.

docs.title: Critical Rules
docs.text:
• Стандартный bolt (когда не указан другой) — 5/8" Dia Titen HD x 5 1/2", заглубление 3 1/2" Emb.
  • Shear walls at foundation часто имеют другой шаг anchor bolts — обязательно проверять SW Schedule (Shear Wall Schedule), не брать стандартный шаг автоматом.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• ATS rod systems are often by others; do not count unless scope says so.
  • Use detail-specific bolt sizes.
  • Keep bolts separate from screws.
  • Washers are not a copy/paste item: update the spacing/count from the current detail instead of leaving the previous spacing unchanged.

docs.text:

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Balcony trims, edge trims, flashing, soffit/underside trims, and related finish material.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Balcony trims should not disappear into general exterior trim.
  • Verify whether details show decking without sheathing.
  • Keep trim, flashing, and structural material separate.
  • Balcony framing can require 2-ply framing and two layers of sleepers; keep framing/sleeper notes visible instead of hiding them in trim.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Railing LF, posts, blocking, attachments, and special trims when in scope.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Deck/porch/balcony railing may be by others; verify scope.
  • Attachment details can drive blocking, bolts, and rim material.

docs.text:
Изображения из Confluence размещены на этой странице по исходной теме. Подпись сохраняет группу-источник, чтобы можно было быстро проверить контекст.

Source group Images Confluence Railing - перила 1 source railing reference 01 (image, 223 KB raw)

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• LVL, PSL, GL, and other beams by size, ply count, and length.
  • Hangers connected to beams.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Mark beams top-down / left-right.
  • Length depends on the bearing/attachment point.
  • Lengths 8' and longer round to 2' increments.
  • Lengths under 8' stay exact.
  • In EWP jobs, ordinary 2x beams are usually excluded.

docs.title: Standard Built-Up Sizes
docs.text: Callout Actual built-up size (2) 1 3/4 x 9 1/2 LVL 3 1/2 x 9 1/2 (3) 1 3/4 x 9 1/2 LVL 5 1/4 x 9 1/2 (4) 1 3/4 x 9 1/2 LVL 7 x 9 1/2

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Steel top nailers or steel beam web fillers can drive separate material lines.
  • Hanger selection depends on face/top/concealed/skewed condition.
  • For Tri-Force and beams with steel plates, write nearest foot sizes; do not leave noisy inch fractions in the size.

docs.title: EWP Materials
docs.text:
Для EWP (Engineered Wood Products) обычные деревянные балки 2x10 / 4x12 / 6x14 не учитываем — только инженерные:

  • LVL (Laminated Veneer Lumber, Versa Lam) — клееный брус, прочный, устойчив к деформациям.
  • PSL (Parallel Strand Lumber) — параллельные волокна, высокая несущая способность.
  • GL (Glued Laminated Timber, Glu Lam) — многослойный клееный брус из ламелей.

docs.title: Output Example
docs.text:
Запись балок и hangers в Excel/PlanSwift (поля: Description, Size, Quantities, Units):

Description Size Qty Units Beam (3) 1 3/4 x 9 1/2 LVL 3 12 Beam (1) 5 1/2 x 11 7/8 GL 1 20 Beam (2) 1 3/4 x 11 7/8 LVL 2 10 Hangers HU412 10 pcs Hangers HGUS7.25/12 20 pcs Hangers GLTV3.514 5 pcs Hangers for 7 x 11 7/8 10 pcs

Длины snap-ятся при scaling — например scaled 12' 0 1/8" округляем до 12.

См. Hangers для подбора крепления под ширину/высоту built-up.

docs.title: Joist
docs.text: Joist runs: keep spacing, rim, blocking, supports, and hangers reviewable.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• I-joists: TJI, LPI, RED, BCI, RFPI, Nordic.
  • Joist hangers.
  • Rim and blocking related to joist runs.
  • Web stiffeners, squash blocks, or special blocking only when called out by details/general notes.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Continue spacing top-down / left-right inside a run.
  • Do not restart spacing from an inside beam.
  • Remove joist only when it lands directly on top of beam, about plus/minus 2".
  • For 18"/20"/22"/24" joists, use HIT hangers, not ITS.
  • ITS is only for light floor applications up to 16".
  • Keep joist depth/product visible in the row name: 11-7/8 TJI 230 is easier to review than a generic TJI joist.
  • Treat top chord bearing conditions separately; they can change ribbon/rim and blocking requirements.

docs.title: Where To Look
docs.text: Drawing area What to verify Framing plan Direction, spacing, depth, product family, repeated areas Beam schedule Support condition and hanger family General notes Web stiffeners, blocking rows, rim material, squash blocks Details Top chord bearing, skewed hangers, firewall conditions

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Add a note when odd lengths are intentional.
  • If joists are top chord bearing, ribbon board may not apply.
  • Check that rim is still counted at roof TJI conditions.
  • Do not split rim into 16' pieces unless the output format specifically asks for pieces.
  • Do not swap joist count and joist length in Excel/output.
  • If the house is about 28' wide and a beam is dropped, verify the joist span labels; joists may be longer than 14'.
  • TJI 9 1/2 does not use 360 / 560 series.
  • Long 2x ceiling joists may be split and imply supporting vertical joists; look for notes/details before assuming one continuous member.

docs.title: EWP Joist Materials
docs.text:
Для EWP учитываем только инженерные joists (обычные деревянные 2x — не считаем):

  • I-Joists (двутавровые, OSB-стенка + LVL/OSB полки) — самый частый вариант.
  • LVL Joists — для больших пролётов, прочные на изгиб.
  • PSL Joists — высокие нагрузки, residential + COM.
  • GL Joists — открытые балки, архитектурные решения.

docs.title: Common I-Joist series
docs.text: TJI · RED · LP / LPI · RFPI · BCI (+ s) · Nordic Joist (NI-20 / NI-40x / NI-60).

docs.title: Standard O.C. Spacing
docs.text:
Шаг (Spacing) меряется on center (O.C.) — от центра одной балки до центра следующей. Стандарты:

  • 12" O.C.
  • 16" O.C.
  • 19.2" O.C.
  • 24" O.C.

docs.title: Output Example
docs.text:
Description Size Qty Units Joists 16" o.c. 11 7/8 TJI 230 3 12 Joists 16" o.c. 11 7/8 TJI 230 36 20 Joists 16" o.c. 11 7/8 TJI 230 2 10 Hangers ITS2.37/11.88 =ЧЁТН(lft * 12/16) pcs

Формула для hangers по joists 16" O.C.: =ЧЁТН(lft * 12 / spacing) — округление вверх до чётного.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Wood posts, steel posts, bearing posts, and built-up post groups when in scope.
  • Post caps/bases only when specifically called out.
  • Posts в крыше — опора для Ridge и Header.

docs.title: Critical Rules
docs.text:
• Posts могут быть цельные (5 1/4 x 5 1/4 PSL, 6x6, 4x4) или сборные из нескольких досок ((2) 2x6, (4) 2x4).
  • Длина Posts очень важна — может идти от фундамента сквозь несколько этажей. Проверяй elevation/section.
  • Post в крыше опирается либо на балку, либо на несущую стену.
  • Указывать кол-во и длину в футах с округлением до 2'.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Material can differ by location: dimensional, LVL, PSL, GL, steel.
  • Posts in panelized wall systems may be by others unless shown as loose structural material.
  • Verify load path details before assuming a post is included in wall panels.
  • Do not create Posts / Post Caps for every built-up beam. Count them only when actual post material is called out, such as 6x6, 4x4, PSL, steel, or another explicit post.
  • If a post is not specified, leave a visible note instead of guessing a post and cap.

docs.title: Connectors
docs.text: Снизу — Post Base к фундаменту/балке. Сверху — Post Cap к балке. Примеры: ABU66, PC46, CCQ66.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Stair stringers, stair platform framing, treads, risers, hangers, and ledgers.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• List 2x12 Tread and add 1x8 Riser for big-job stair tread output.
  • Stair platform ledgers at ground level can be PT attached to CMU.
  • Joist hangers for stair framing belong inside the stair takeoff section.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• DHU/DGU can apply at stair firewall hanger conditions; mark separately.
  • Stair/elevator/shaft hangers should not be mixed with regular demising hangers.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Subfloor sheathing by assembly.
  • Plywood underlayment.
  • FRT perimeter strips.
  • Sound membrane/gypcrete-related layers when in scope.

docs.title: Common Miss
docs.text: Floor assembly underlayment can be huge. Always check assemblies, not only plan views.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• 2' or 4' FRT perimeter notes.
  • Underlayment over slab.
  • Deck and balcony underlayment assemblies.
  • Blocking below FRT perimeter sheathing where details show it.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Wall blocking, joist blocking, drywall blocking, roof/floor blocking, and special detail blocking.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Walls 10' and above need two rows of blocking.
  • Blocking stays in LFT unless pieces are required.
  • Blocking has no factor when continuous, unless job-specific output says otherwise.
  • Exterior blocking is FRT when exterior walls are FRT.
  • Bracing is typically 2x4 unless a detail says otherwise.
  • For truss bracing, count every 10' as blocking when the note says Trusses - Bracing 2x6.
  • I-joist cross bridging / TB27: use length * 2 * 12 / 16 pcs.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Drywall blocking/ledger at demising and exterior walls parallel with framing.
  • Kitchen and bath blocking.
  • Blocking under FRT subfloor perimeter.
  • Parapet FRT blocking.
  • If EWP are by others but blocking for steel beams is called out, keep the LVL / LSL blocking note visible and scaled.

docs.title: Formulas
docs.text:
Use project-specific Excel syntax, but the logic from notes is:

Flat 48" o.c.     = G * 12 / 48 * 2 * 1.1 / D, ceiling to 1
Diagonal 48" o.c. = G * 12 / 48 * 2.5 * 1.1 / D, ceiling to 1

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Make sure 1.1 is not already included upstream.
  • Use 16' ceiling for ribbons, bracings, and ledgers when required.
  • Keep plates and wall blocking in LFT when that is the expected output.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Bolts, anchor bolts, holdown bolts, and detail-specific bolts.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Count bolts and Simpson screws where details require them.
  • Do not count nails by default; they buy boxes and the quantity becomes noise.
  • Washers for filler steel anchors do not need to be listed as 3" square unless details require that; a plain washer note can be enough.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Do not use the same label for a screw detail and a bolt detail.
  • ATS rod systems can be by others; verify before counting.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• 2x4 bracing at exterior, interior carrying, and demising walls where typical workflow requires it.
  • Drywall ledger at demising walls both sides and exterior walls one side when parallel with framing.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Bracing lengths by wall height:
Wall height Bracing length 16'-20' 20' 12'-16' 18' 10'-12' 16' Other 14'

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Dropped soffits are not deflection-track walls.
  • RCP pages often show bracing/soffit framing scope.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Ribbon board where joists/trusses require it and details show it.

docs.title: Rule
docs.text: Top chord bearing trusses do not need 2x4 ribbon board. In those conditions, use blocking between trusses instead, often (2) 2x6 over 2x6 bearing walls.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Do not confuse ribbon board, rim, and blocking.
  • If trusses hang over walls, check whether blocking or drywall ledger is the actual material.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Do not write 1-3/4 LVL Rim unless LVL is explicitly called out or needed by detail.
  • Default output can be 11-7/8" Rim.
  • If LSL is specified, write 11-7/8" LSL Rim.
  • Rim factor is 1.05 in LFT.
  • Roof TJI needs rim too.

docs.title: Typical Products
docs.text: Product When 1-1/8" OSB Rim Common thin rim 1-1/4" or 1-1/2" LSL Rim When LSL or stronger rim is specified 1-3/4" LVL Rim Deck/corridor frame or explicit detail

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
If product is assumed, say assumed in the note.

At demising conditions, extra rim may be required on both sides. Do not count only one side when the detail/plan implies both sides of the demising line.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Simpson screws and structural screws called out by details.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Screws are counted when details specifically call them out.
  • Do not mix screw and bolt detail labels.
  • Nails are usually excluded.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Detail 1 and detail 3 may look similar but use different fasteners.
  • Keep screw notes close to the related connection item.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Wood fillers, nailers, bolts/screws/washers, and attachment material at steel beams where shown.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Steel top nailers can drive top-mount hanger conditions.
  • Filler steel anchors may need washers, but do not over-specify washer size unless called out.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Hangers may bear on top of steel or wood nailer; this changes hanger type.
  • Keep assumptions visible.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Canopy framing, soffit sheathing, exterior-grade gypsum/Denseglas, posts, beams, and attachments.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Canopies and porch soffits may need 5/8" exterior-grade gypsum soffit sheathing.
  • RCP and architectural sheets often show canopy soffit framing.
  • Metal stud joists may be required at gazebo/canopy conditions.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Double/triple rafters and built-up roof members.

docs.title: Critical Rules
docs.text:
• Dbl/Trpl Rafters работают параллельно Rafters, как внутренняя усиленная балка крыши.
  • Сечения и материалы — любые: 1 3/4 x 11 7/8 LVL, 2x12, (2) 2x10 и т.д.
  • Опирание — на Ridge, перекрытие или стену.
  • Длина как у обычных Rafters — всегда сверять по разрезам и фасадам, не брать с плана.
  • Указывать кол-во и длину в футах с округлением до 2'.
  • Hangers нужны только если Dbl/Trpl Rafters опираются на несущий Ridge, либо это явно указано на плане. См. Hangers.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Product can be dimensional lumber, LVL, PSL, or GL.
  • Built-up member length and ply count must both be visible in output.
  • Use hangers/connectors matching the actual built-up width.

docs.text: Name Size Count Length / pcs Dbl Rafters 2x12 2 18 Trpl Rafters 1 3/4 x 11 7/8 LVL 3 28 Connector Size Count Unit Hangers LSSR2.56Z 2 pcs Hangers LRU212Z 1 pcs

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Dormer walls, roof framing, sheathing, trims, and openings as separate scope when shown.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Dormers often combine wall, roof, opening, and trim rules.
  • Verify exterior sheathing/WRB and FRT rules.
  • Do not hide dormer materials inside generic roof lines if pricing needs them separate.

docs.text:

Source group Images Confluence Dormer, Shed Dormer (надстроенная крыша) 1 source dormer framing reference 01 (image, 182 KB raw)

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Roof opening headers, dormer/canopy headers, and support beams.

docs.title: Critical Rules
docs.text:
• Header (в крыше) — горизонтальная балка в горизонтальных стыках крыши или внутренняя балка. Не путать с оконной перемычкой (window header).
  • Может быть одинарной, двойной или тройной балкой: 1 3/4 x 11 7/8 LVL, 2x12, (2) 2x10.
  • Опирание Header — на Dbl/Trpl Rafters или на Posts.
  • Длина Header определяется между Dbl/Trpl Rafters.
  • Указывать кол-во и длину в футах с округлением до 2'.
  • Header требует Hangers — крепления подбираются по ширине и высоте built-up.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Header sizes may differ by roof area or floor.
  • If wall panels include headers, avoid double-counting.
  • Hanger type depends on face/top/skewed condition.

docs.title: Output Tables
docs.text: Name Size Count Length / pcs Header (2) 2x12 2 10 Header (3) 1 3/4 x 11 7/8 LVL 3 12 Connector Size Count Unit Hangers LUS210-2 1 pcs Hangers HU5.50/10 1 pcs

docs.text:

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Hip rafters/beams and related connectors when the scope includes roof framing.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Confirm whether hips are stick-framed or truss by others.
  • Engineered hip beams need exact product and length.
  • Keep hip material separate from roof trim if output pricing differs.

docs.text:

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Overframing members, sleepers, sheathing, and attachment details.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Piggy trusses are split upper trusses; count 2x6 sleepers between parts when shown.
  • Overframes can hide a lot of material in details, not plan view.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Roof too tall / split truss conditions.
  • Sleeper spacing and length.
  • Cover board and roof layers above overframed areas.

docs.title: Confluence Images
docs.text:
Изображения из Confluence размещены на этой странице по исходной теме. Подпись сохраняет группу-источник, чтобы можно было быстро проверить контекст.

Source group Images Confluence Overframes (скрытая части крыши) 3 source overframe reference 01 (image, 180 KB raw) overframe reference 02 (image, 143 KB raw) overframe reference 03 (image, 143 KB raw)

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Ridge beams/boards when in scope.
  • Hangers or connectors tied to ridge framing.

docs.title: Critical Rules
docs.text:
• Ridge — самый верхний элемент крыши.
  • Может быть одинарной, двойной или тройной балкой разного материала и сечения: 1 3/4 x 11 7/8 LVL, 2x12, (2) 2x10.
  • Опирание Ridge — на стены Gable или на Posts.
  • Длина Ridge определяется от внешней грани экстерьерной стены (не от грани wall framing внутри).
  • Указывать кол-во и длину в футах с округлением до 2'.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Determine whether ridge is engineered wood, dimensional lumber, or truss by others.
  • Roof framing can include TJI rim/blocking just like floor framing.

docs.title: Output Table
docs.text: Name Size Count Length Ridge 2x12 1 10 Ridge (2) 1 3/4 x 11 7/8 LVL 2 12

docs.text:

Source group Images Confluence Ridge (конек) 1 source ridge reference 01 (image, 189 KB raw)

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Roof OSB/Plywood.
  • Flat roof cover board, Densedeck/glass mat, rigid insulation, and XPS layers.
  • Soffit plywood under roof trusses where shown.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Flat roof trusses often need Densedeck over the same area as rigid insulation.
  • Extra rigid XPS layer is a recurring miss.
  • Piggy truss sleepers can be hidden in roof details.
  • 1x3 strapping under roof trusses may be required.
  • For roofs framed with AJS Rafters, count rafters manually instead of Rake when the condition behaves like an exposed floor overhang.
  • Some details call for every other rafter to be doubled; do not average this into a generic rafter run.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Valley rafters/beams, valley framing, and connectors where shown.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Valley framing can be part of overframe or truss package by others.
  • If stick-framed, check sheathing, blocking, and roof finish interfaces.

docs.title: Base
docs.text: Baseboard — measure the perimeter, then subtract excluded segments.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Wood base marked Wd, Wood, or equivalent in room schedules.
  • Unit base separately from corridors, lobby, and other common areas.
  • Floor-by-floor quantities when finish schedules differ.

docs.title: Exclude
docs.text:
• Tile base when the scope asks for wood base.
  • Rubber/vinyl/metal base unless specifically part of the trim scope.
  • Rooms where finish schedule clearly says no wood base.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Room schedule may have multiple finish columns; verify the base column rather than relying on room name.
  • Bathrooms often have tile base and should be excluded from wood-base counts.
  • Common areas must be separated because they may use another base type.

docs.title: Trello Rules
docs.text: Rule What to check Source No Baseboard behind kitchen cabinets. Remove cabinet-backed wall segments from base formula. Trello Baseboard is present below windows unless the finish notes say otherwise. Do not accidentally skip short under-window runs. Trello No Baseboard where stair trim/wall junction makes it not applicable. Check stair-to-wall intersections visually. Trello Baseboard is counted in toilets and shower rooms when shown by finish scope. Verify against base material, because tile base may override wood base. Trello No Baseboard in non-living rooms or rooms without finish. Exclude unfinished / service spaces unless schedule says otherwise. Trello In Excel, write all measured values into the formula. Keep formula inspectable, not just a final number. Trello Check material such as TBD Base. Leave a visible note if material is not specified. Trello

docs.title: Takeoff Method
docs.text:
• Baseboard and crowns are measured by perimeter. Start at the entry door and trace continuously so breaks are intentional, not accidental.
  • Bathroom / shower-room base can be separated from the main base quantity when review needs it.
  • Typical no-base spaces: garage, mechanical/equipment rooms, crawl space, and other unfinished rooms.

docs.title: Visual Examples
docs.text: No base behind kitchen cabinets Base below windows No stair-wall junction base Check toilets / shower rooms No finish / non-living rooms Excel formula values

docs.title: Output Notes
docs.text: If base type is unclear, keep a note such as Base not specified in room schedule instead of assigning a material.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Door casing.
  • Window casing.
  • Interior jamb trim where the estimating method uses casing.

docs.title: Local Rule
docs.text:
For interior door jambs, local feedback says casing can be used divided by 2:

Interior door jamb trim = casing / 2

Use this only where it matches the current template/scope.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Door schedule may provide size/material/fire rating, but not trim type.
  • Separate interior casing from exterior trims and flashing.
  • Do not count hardware numbers as trim.

docs.title: Output
docs.text: Keep casing grouped by location/type if common areas differ from units.

docs.title: Crown
docs.text: Crown — count only where scope and room condition allow it.

docs.title: Rule
docs.text: Crowns should be included when interior trim scope is active.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Crown molding by room/area where shown or required by the trim scope.
  • Separate common areas from units when the finish schedule differs.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Crown may appear in finish notes instead of plan view.
  • Do not confuse crown with exterior cornice, fascia, frieze, or rake trim.
  • If trim scope exists but crown is unclear, add a visible note.
  • Typical no-crown spaces: garage, mechanical/equipment rooms, crawl space, and other rooms without finish.

docs.title: Trello Rules
docs.text: Rule What to check Source No Crowns in closets. Exclude Closet / CLO. spaces unless scope says otherwise. Trello No Crowns in rooms with sloped ceilings. Watch rooms marked slope ceiling / sloped roof conditions. Trello No Crowns in rooms without finish. Exclude unfinished areas. Trello Crowns are counted in bathrooms when trim scope requires it. Do not automatically exclude bathrooms. Trello On the second level, count crowns only in Hall, foyer, and large guest rooms where required. Separate second-floor crown logic from first-floor living areas. Trello In Excel, write all measured values into the formula. Keep the formula reviewable. Trello Check material such as TBD Crowns. Leave a visible note if material is not specified. Trello

docs.title: Takeoff Method
docs.text:
• Measure crowns by the same room perimeter logic as baseboard.
  • Write each measured segment into the Excel formula, not only the final total.
  • If the crown material is TBD, keep the material note visible.

docs.title: Visual Examples
docs.text: No crowns in closets No slope-ceiling crowns No finish rooms Bathrooms can have crowns Second level: Hall / foyer / guest Excel formula values

docs.title: Door and Window Trim
docs.text: Interior doors and cased openings — read plan tags, convert sizes, then fill Excel.

docs.title: Door Trim
docs.text:
• Unit entry doors may be fire-rated and should be labeled clearly in door takeoff, but hardware numbers are not needed for trim.
  • Interior door jamb trim can use casing divided by 2 where the local method applies.
  • Separate common-area doors from unit doors if trim type differs.

docs.title: Window Trim
docs.text:
• Count casing/stool/apron only if the finish schedule or details include them.
  • Do not reuse exterior window trim logic for interior trims without checking scope.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Door/window trim can be hidden in finish schedules, interior elevations, or details rather than framing sheets.
  • Keep trim notes separate from blocking/jamb framing notes.
  • If the schedule names a manufacturer or special product, keep it in the note instead of flattening the opening to a generic size.

docs.title: Trello Rules
docs.text: Rule What to check Source Measure interior doors by the open door symbol. Count the actual door swing/opening, not nearby wall notes. Trello Door size 2680 means width 2'-6" and height 8'-0". Keep size notation consistent before Excel conversion. Trello Double doors are written like (2)2680. Keep the pair marker visible. Trello Pocket doors are written like 2680 Pocket. Preserve the Pocket note. Trello Slider doors are written like (2)3080 Slider. Preserve both quantity and type. Trello Garage/mechanical metal doors can be F.R S.C.. Note fire-rated and self-close conditions. Trello Do not enter glass shower doors as interior doors. Exclude shower glass doors from interior door count. Trello Large guest rooms may use French doors. Keep glass/French door note visible. Trello Cased Openings are openings without doors. Write cased openings separately from doors. Trello 4080 C.O. is a cased-opening notation. Convert and enter it in the cased-opening side, not doors. Trello Enter interior doors and cased openings for each level in Excel. Separate floors before trim formulas. Trello If a door schedule exists, fill from the schedule. Prefer schedule data over manual plan-only entry. Trello

docs.title: Excel Entry
docs.text:
• After all doors/openings are written, copy the opening mark and quantity columns into the right-side Excel helper table for casing formulas.
  • Rewrite all door and cased-opening sizes into the left table in inches: 2'-0" x 6'-8" becomes 24 x 80.
  • For many repeated doors, use an Excel formula instead of manual repeated typing.
  • Before output, select the entered door/opening cells and check the status-bar sum against the takeoff count.

docs.title: Visual Examples
docs.text: Measure by open door Size notation: 2680 Double door: (2)2680 Pocket door Slider door Cased opening Size conversion to inches Use door schedule

docs.title: Interior Trims
docs.text:
Interior Trims — PDF sheets, perimeter measurement, and Excel output flow.

Interior trims are separate from exterior trims, drywall, and framing. Use this section when the scope includes finish trim items from room schedules, door/window schedules, or finish plans.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Base / wood base.
  • Door casing and jamb trim.
  • Window casing, stool, apron, and related trim if shown.
  • Crown when trim scope is active.
  • Common-area trims separately from unit trims.

docs.title: Do Not Mix
docs.text:
• Exterior casing, mullion, sill, head, corner board, watertable, siding trim.
  • Gypsum board for walls.
  • Framing blocking, unless the trim requires backing and the scope asks for it.

docs.title: Core Rules
docs.text:
• If trims are not specified, write not specified.
  • Room schedules can have tile base; exclude tile base when the takeoff needs wood base only.
  • Common areas such as corridors and lobbies should be separate because trim type can differ.
  • Crowns should be included when interior trim scope is active.
  • First make the interior-trim PDFs/sheets, then measure baseboard and crowns by continuous room perimeter.
  • Casing for interior doors is filled after the interior-door list is entered; casing for exterior doors/windows is filled after exterior openings are entered.

  • Are corridors/lobbies a different base or casing type?
  • Is crown required everywhere, only common areas, or only specific rooms?

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Eve framing, soffit frames, soffit plywood/gypsum, subfascia, and trim where shown.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Eve soffit frames can be 2x6, not 2x4, if drawings show it.
  • If 30% or more of soffit is beadboard, count the full soffit SQFT as beadboard when using that local rule.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• RCP and architectural sections can show soffits missing from structural plans.
  • Vinyl Soffits / Soffit Eve Vinyl belong in SQFT, not as a framing line.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Exterior opening flashing, wall/roof flashing, deck/balcony flashing, and related jamb logic.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Exterior door/window jamb quantities can use flashing-style formulas.
  • If the template separates trims and flashing, keep them separate.

docs.title: Check
docs.text: • Rake/eve/parapet trim and flashing may be called out on architectural sheets, not structural.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Rake trim, sheathing edge treatment, and related fascia/subfascia.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Keep rake separate from eve and returns when the output template expects separate lines.
  • Confirm FRT/exterior treatment for exposed wood.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Roof/eave returns, framing, trim, and sheathing.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Returns often appear in architectural details rather than structural plans.
  • Separate returns from rake/eve if pricing lines differ.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Ridge, valley, and hip framing/trim/materials where the scope requires them.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Keep roof framing members separate from roof finish trim if the output template prices them separately.
  • Verify whether members are dimensional lumber, LVL/PSL/GL, or truss by others.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Asphalt, metal, flat roof, and alternate roof systems only when in scope.
  • Cover boards, rigid insulation, XPS, glass mat, and Densedeck for flat roofs.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Plans may show asphalt shingles and standing seam metal alternates.
  • Flat roof trusses often need Densedeck/cover board over the same area as rigid insulation.
  • Piggy trusses require sleeper review.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Floor areas by level.
  • Assemblies that drive subfloor, underlayment, sound mat, gypcrete, or other material.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Keep floors separate even when identical.
  • Add a note when a floor is identical to another floor, but list materials separately.
  • Check lower bearing wall notes; 1st/2nd floor walls may need double studs.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Balcony decking, trims, sleepers, framing, railings, and attachment details.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Details may show decking without sheathing.
  • Balcony trims should be separated from general exterior trims.
  • Attachment to building can drive LVL rim, bolts, or screws.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Basement floor/wall areas that drive sheathing, sleepers, insulation, or finish assemblies.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Basement SQFT plywood and sleepers are easy to skip.
  • Under slab / over slab assemblies may still call for underlayment.
  • Existing/podium conditions should not be treated as standard panels without checking details.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Cantilevered floor/roof/deck areas and their sheathing, blocking, insulation, and rim conditions.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Cantilever conditions often change rim/edge material.
  • Exterior FRT rules may apply.
  • Verify details for extra blocking, bracing, or holdowns.
  • Keep the visible label Cantilevered in the output so the reviewer can see why the SQFT is separated.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Decking, sleepers, sheathing/underlayment, framing, railings, trims, and attachment details when in scope.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Deck assemblies can call for 1/2" underlayment.
  • LVL rim is only used when specifically required for deck/corridor strength.
  • Exterior attachment details may require bolts or Simpson screws.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Loft floor area.
  • Related guards, stairs, openings, and roof interface materials when shown.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Loft areas can be hidden on architectural sheets while framing details live on structural sheets.
  • Confirm whether loft framing is floor framing, roof framing, or by others.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Porch framing, soffit sheathing, decking, posts, beams, and railings when in scope.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Some COM jobs rarely include porch/deck scope; verify rather than assuming.
  • Exterior soffit sheathing can be 5/8" exterior-grade gypsum or similar Denseglas product.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Roof sheathing area.
  • Flat roof insulation, cover board, and protection board where shown.
  • Roof truss accessories by perimeter/detail.

docs.title: Common Misses
docs.text:
• 1/4" Densedeck or 1/2" Glass Mat protection board.
  • Additional rigid XPS layer.
  • Piggy truss sleepers.
  • Roof TJI rim.
  • 1x3 strapping under roof trusses when specified.

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/65339407/Headers

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Exterior and interior headers when they are part of the takeoff scope.
  • Flush headers even in panelized jobs when they are not included in panels.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• In panelized wall jobs, exterior and interior wall headers often belong with Walls, not Floor Framing.
  • Do not count panelized wall headers if wall panels already include them.
  • Header sizes can change by floor; check schedules carefully.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Higher floors may have larger exterior headers than lower floors.
  • Structural schedules override architectural wall assumptions.
  • (3) means a three-ply header; do not enter only one board for a triple header.

docs.title: Windows and Doors
docs.text: Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/65339393/Windows+Doors

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Window and door openings by type/size.
  • Jamb blocking for all windows and interior doors.
  • Fire-rated unit entry doors from corridors.

docs.title: Door Labels
docs.text:
• Use material and fire rating where relevant: 2670 FCW, 3070 HM C-lbl.
  • Unit entries can be labeled 3070 Entry.
  • Hardware numbers usually are not needed in the takeoff list.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Exterior jambs can use flashing-style formulas.
  • Interior door jambs can use casing divided by 2 where that is the local method.
  • Room schedule tile base should be excluded if only wood base is needed.

docs.title: Flashing Table
docs.text:
Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/65044582/Openings

Opening item Output Window Flashing Sill Flashing

docs.title: PlanSwift Marking & Macro
docs.text:
• Подсчёт всех openings — макрос F_Openings (даёт Window Flashing + Sill Flashing).
  • У всех дверей обязательно ставь пометку d.
  • У гаражных дверей — пометка gd.
  • Если окно и дверь объединены одним хэдером (например, sliding patio в blocks с фиксом сбоку) — считай их вместе одним openings, ставь d.
  • Все openings вырезаются из sheathing — не забывай subtract при подсчёте Wall Sheathing SQFT.

docs.text:
Изображения из Confluence размещены на этой странице по исходной теме. Подпись сохраняет группу-источник, чтобы можно было быстро проверить контекст.

docs.title: Count
docs.text: • Box-only sheathing areas around floor/roof transitions, parapets, and loose wall conditions.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Do not label box sheathing as truss heel.
  • For panelized wall jobs, box sheathing can remain in scope even when wall panels are by others.
  • Optional walls may require full-height sheathing instead of box-only.
  • Tilda wall checklist says to check hidden box sheathing in sections/details, not only in plan view.
  • At attic conditions, add floor height to box sheathing only for the first height when the local rule calls for it.
  • Attic subfloor can be built from extra plate + box = subfloor; do not miss the subfloor line when the box/plate detail is the clue.

docs.title: Check
docs.text: • Match labels to details; copied detail labels are a recurring error.

docs.title: Duplicate of Gable
docs.text: This page is kept because the current takeoff tool tree has this item. Use it only when the job template has a separate duplicated gable bucket.

docs.title: Rule
docs.text: Do not double-count gable sheathing. If this item is used, add a note explaining which gable area is here and which area is in regular Gable Sheathing.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Floor sheathing and underlayment per assembly.
  • FRT perimeter strips where specified.
  • Sound membrane or multi-layer floor assemblies.

docs.title: Common Miss
docs.text: 1/2" plywood underlayment per floor assembly can be a very large quantity. Do not rely only on structural floor framing plans; check assemblies and details.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• FRT 2' or 4' perimeter notes.
  • Underlayment over slab when assembly calls for it.
  • Deck underlayment assemblies.

docs.title: Confluence Images
docs.text:
Изображения из Confluence размещены на этой странице по исходной теме. Подпись сохраняет группу-источник, чтобы можно было быстро проверить контекст.

docs.title: Gable Sheathing
docs.text: Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/89948176/Gable

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Gable OSB/Plywood SQFT where shown.
  • WRB, siding substrate, and trim if in scope.
  • Sheathing area for exterior, interior, porch, and dormer gables.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Do not mix this with truss heel or box sheathing.
  • Exterior sheathing thickness follows Arch/energy notes unless Structural gives stronger non-Zip requirement.
  • FRT follows exterior wall rules.
  • For truss gables, verify whether sheathing is loose material while studs/plates are included in trusses.

docs.title: Shear Wall
docs.text: Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/90210306/Shear+Wall+Sheathing

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Shear wall sheathing per structural schedule.
  • Holdowns, A35 clips, and related details where required.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Demising shear wall sheathing may be only one side or half the wall; follow the schedule.
  • FRT is not automatic for demising shear wall sheathing.
  • Interior sheathing follows Structural.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• A35 clips at shear wall connection to another shear wall below.
  • Holdowns per S-details.
  • Interior vs exterior sheathing thickness.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Vertical sheathing at the heel of roof trusses.
  • Related blocking or exterior wall treatment where shown.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Keep Truss Heel separate from Box Sheathing.
  • In panelized COM jobs, truss heel sheathing can still be loose material.
  • Check if top chord bearing truss condition removes ribbon board and requires blocking between trusses.

docs.title: Typical Heel Height
docs.text:
• Стандартная высота truss heel ≈ 1'-7" (берётся от верха стены до верха верхнего пояса фермы по краю).
  • Используй это как sanity-check, если на чертеже высота heel не подписана; конкретное число — всегда из truss-package, если он есть.

docs.title: Где не пропустить heel sheathing
docs.text:
• На gable-сторонах heel-обшивка обычно уже учтена в Gable Sheathing (треугольник + полоска heel под ним идут одной плоскостью).
  • На Eave-сторонах (длинная сторона свеса) heel-обшивка часто забывается — это отдельная вертикальная полоска по периметру здания между Top Plate стены и нижом overhang. Проверяй её отдельной строкой.

docs.title: Confluence Images
docs.text:
Изображения из Confluence размещены на этой странице по исходной теме. Подпись сохраняет группу-источник, чтобы можно было быстро проверить контекст.

docs.title: Wall Sheathing
docs.text:
Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/90144770/Wall+Sheathing

Wall sheathing priority: Arch/Energy can control exterior, Structural controls shear.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Exterior sheathing by Arch / energy / Zip notes.
  • Interior shear wall sheathing by Structural.
  • Loose/box/full-height sheathing separately when project scope distinguishes it.
  • Densglass, FRT, Zip, plywood/OSB, and gypsum-based sheathing as separate lines when the drawings call out different products.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• 19/32" is 5/8", not 1/2".
  • Zip on exterior walls supersedes structural sheathing notes, but keep a note.
  • Non-Zip exception: if Arch says 1/2" and Structural says 5/8", use 5/8" for strength.
  • Optional walls may need full-height sheathing; loose sheathing may be box only.
  • Interior shear wall sheathing follows the shear wall schedule, including one-side vs both-sides requirements.
  • Do not hide sheathing inside generic wall SQFT when the reviewer needs product, thickness, side, or location.

docs.title: Source Priority
docs.text: Situation Default takeoff decision Exterior wall has Zip note Use Zip; keep Structural conflict as note Exterior wall has no Zip, Arch 1/2" vs Structural 5/8" Use 5/8" Shear wall schedule says both sides Count both sides, not wall area once Wall type/elevation calls Densglass Separate Densglass by level/elevation FRT exterior wall material Check if sheathing/blocking/parapet also changes

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• FRT sheathing notes at exterior walls.
  • Densglass at metal walls or specific elevations.
  • Shear wall schedule one-side vs both-sides requirements.
  • Full-height vs box-only sheathing at optional walls.
  • Floor-height sheathing around panelized COM jobs where loose material is still in scope.
  • Draft stop sheathing at party/demising walls is not the same as shear wall sheathing. Keep draft stop and shear wall quantities separate.
  • Draft stop can appear at walls and between floors; count each supplied scope in the matching section rather than combining it into one wall line.

docs.title: Sheathing Material Variants
docs.text:
Стандартные продукты, которые встречаются на чертежах:

Запись на чертеже Что брать 1/2" CDX Ply Plywood CDX, 1/2" 1/2" OSB OSB, 1/2" 1/2" Ply (или просто APA RATED) Plywood APA-rated, 1/2" 7/16" Zip Huber Zip System, 7/16" Zip Tape Лента Zip — отдельной строкой к Zip-обшивке
  • Если на плане только APA RATED без указания материала — это Plywood, не OSB.
  • Zip Tape идёт только в паре с Zip-обшивкой; не забывай добавить отдельную строку.

docs.title: Confluence Images
docs.text:
Изображения из Confluence размещены на этой странице по исходной теме. Подпись сохраняет группу-источник, чтобы можно было быстро проверить контекст.

Source group Images Confluence Sheathing 2 source wall sheathing reference 01 (preview, 1299 KB raw) wall sheathing reference 02 (preview, 5480 KB raw)

docs.title: Corners
docs.text: Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/65273871/Corners

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Corner studs, extra studs, holdowns, and structural corner details when called out.
  • Sheathing/trim conditions that differ at corners.

docs.title: Critical Rules
docs.text:
• Wall stick — внешние углы необходимо подсчитать отдельной позицией.
  • Wall panels — углы НЕ считаем (они уже входят в panel).
  • Считай углы по этажам — corners by floor должны выдаваться отдельной строкой.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Structural details override generic wall assumptions.
  • If corners belong to panelized walls, verify what is in the loose material scope.
  • Keep notes visible when corner material is assumed rather than specified.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Corridor studs, plates, blocking, bracing, fire-rated openings, and relevant sheathing.
  • Corridor ledgers or drywall ledger where floor framing runs parallel.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Corridor studs can be staggered: 2x4 staggered usually means two rows at 16" o.c. with 2x6 plates.
  • Unit entry doors from corridors are fire rated; label them clearly.
  • Corridor spans are often framed with 2x10 on ledger; verify structural plans.

docs.title: Check
docs.text: • Do not use DHU/DGU hangers just because wall is demising/corridor. Use DHU/DGU only where joists hang over true fire wall conditions per details.

docs.title: Что считается corridor wall
docs.text:
Corridor walls — это не только стены вдоль коридоров. Сюда же относятся стены, разделяющие жилые помещения от нежилых:

  • между лестницей (stair) и жилым помещением;
  • между гаражом и жилым помещением;
  • любые fire-rated перегородки common-area от unit-зоны.

Запись в PlanSwift — cor 2x6 x / corr 2x6 x (см. Exterior → PlanSwift Wall Names). Двойной коридор — cor (2) 2x6 x.

docs.title: Confluence Images
docs.text:
Изображения из Confluence размещены на этой странице по исходной теме. Подпись сохраняет группу-источник, чтобы можно было быстро проверить контекст.

Source group Images Confluence Corridor (коридорные стены) 1 source corridor wall reference 01 (image, 67 KB raw)

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Demising wall studs, plates, blocking, bracing, sheathing, and drywall ledgers when in scope.
  • Double demising walls separately.

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Some demising walls are double; do not average them into regular walls.
  • Shear wall sheathing at demising walls may be one side only or half the wall length; follow the schedule.
  • Regular demising hanger conditions usually use ITS, not DHU/DGU, when gypsum stops below floor level.

docs.title: Check
docs.text:
• Drywall ledger: 2x4 at both sides of demising walls where parallel with framing.
  • Double studs on lower bearing levels if structural notes call for them.
  • Draft stop sheathing at party/demising walls is separate from shear wall sheathing. If structural details call for draft stop, count it at the wall and at floor-framing levels as separate measurements.
  • Do not assume draft stop material is P.T.; use regular material unless the detail/spec explicitly calls for P.T.

docs.title: PlanSwift Записи (двойные demising)
docs.text:
Для demising стен с двумя рядами стоек указывай обе толщины и обе высоты, если они отличаются:

Запись Что значит dem (2) 2x6 10.5 Двойной demising 2x6, обе стены высотой 10.5' dem 2x4 2x6 9 10.5 Двойной demising: одна стена 2x4 высотой 9', вторая 2x6 высотой 10.5' dem 2x4 2x4 10.5 9 Двойной demising 2x4/2x4 разной высоты dem 2x6 2x6 11 12 Двойной demising 2x6/2x6 разной высоты

Если обе стены идентичны — короткая запись dem (2) 2x6 10.5. Если стены разные — пиши толщины подряд, потом высоты подряд.

docs.text:

Source group Images Confluence Demising (меж-секционные стены - двойные) 1 source demising wall reference 01 (image, 110 KB raw)

docs.title: Exterior Walls
docs.text: Exterior wall takeoff: split the wall into layers before counting materials.

docs.title: Count
docs.text:
• Studs, plates, blocking, sheathing, bracing, Tyvek/WRB, insulation, and exterior buildouts when in scope.
  • For panelized COM jobs, count only loose/exterior scope such as Tyvek, bracing, floor-height sheathing, box sheathing, truss heel, and parapet items.
  • Residential/Tilda checklist buckets: basement exterior walls, first floor exterior walls, second floor exterior walls, attic/loft exterior walls, chimney walls, knee walls, dormer walls, garage walls, and balloon walls.

docs.title: Critical Rules
docs.text:
• Exterior sheathing follows Arch / energy / Zip notes unless Structural gives a stronger non-Zip requirement.
  • If exterior wall material is FRT, exterior blocking and parapets are FRT too.
  • Exterior buildouts are stick framed, not panels.
  • For one-hour exterior walls, provide exterior wall SQFT when requested.
  • Check wall size by floor: 2x4, 2x6, or 2x8.
  • Bottom plates may need to be separated when the lower plate is P.T.

docs.title: Wall Sizing & Height
docs.text:
• На плане толщина стен: 5 1/2" для 2x6, 3 1/2" для 2x4.
  • Высота стены — от нижней доски Btm Plate до верхних досок Top Plates (включая обе доски). Брать с elevation или section, не с плана.
  • В PlanSwift запись: ext <толщина> <высота>, например ext 2x6 9.0.

docs.title: Под Bottom Plate (на бетоне)
docs.text:
При посадке экстерьерной стены на бетон/фундамент в takeoff попадают три позиции — их легко забыть, потому что на плане они не показаны:

  • Sill Sealer — поролоновая прокладка между бетоном и нижней доской.
  • Termite Shield — металлическая защита от термитов между бетоном и Btm Plate.
  • Washers — шайбы под анкерные болты Btm Plate к фундаменту.

См. Anchor Bolts и Sill Plates для bolts/plates конкретики.

docs.title: Where To Look
docs.text: Drawing area What to pull from it Wall type schedule Stud size, spacing, fire rating, sheathing notes Exterior elevations Special sheathing/Densglass zones, material changes, gables Energy / envelope sheets Zip, WRB, rigid insulation, continuous insulation Structural plans/details Bracing, shear wall notes, holdowns, tall-wall blocking RCP / soffit details Dropped exterior soffit frames and buildouts

docs.title: Takeoff Output
docs.text:
• Keep exterior wall framing, sheathing, WRB, insulation, and buildouts as separate rows when the scope or pricing needs review.
  • Note assumptions directly in the output: 2x6 ext wall assumed from wall type is better than silently mixing sizes.
  • If a floor repeats, still list that floor separately and add an same as note instead of combining floors.

docs.title: PlanSwift Wall Names
docs.text:
Source: https://ewood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/work/pages/65175555/Walls

Wall output name Use for ext x Exterior wall run cor 2x6 x / corr 2x6 x Corridor wall, 2x6 cor 2x4 x / corr 2x4 x Corridor wall, 2x4 dem 2x6 x Demising wall, 2x6 dem 2x4 x Demising wall, 2x4 cor (2) 2x6 x / corr (2) 2x6 x Double corridor wall, 2x6 cor (2) 2x4 x / corr (2) 2x4 x Double corridor wall, 2x4 dem (2) 2x6 x Double demising wall, 2x6 dem (2) 2x4 x Double demising wall, 2x4 2x4 x / 2x6 x Generic wall run by stud size 2x4 half / 2x6 half Half-height wall by stud size

docs.title: Common Misses
docs.text:
• Rigid insulation.
  • R6 Zip wall system.
  • 5/8" Densglass on levels or elevations called out by wall type schedule.
  • Dropped soffit frames from RCP pages.
  • Garage doors quantity on first-floor/garage wall takeoff.
  • Corner studs and corners by floor.
  • Exterior blocking changing to FRT because the exterior wall material is FRT.
  • Parapet framing that follows the exterior wall material rule.
